Kamino xStocks — weekend comparator: 2026-04-24 → 2026-04-27

Forward-running comparator that tracks how Soothsayer's closed-market signal maps into Kamino's actually-deployed xStock reserve buffers and near-threshold borrower states. All 8 xStocks live in one lending market and consume Scope as primary oracle. Reserve config snapshot from 2026-04-27. Reproducible from the on-chain klend program.

SymbolLTV/liqFri closeMon openRealized (bps)Soothsayer τ=0.85 cov / hw bpsSoothsayer τ=0.95 cov / hw bpsKamino near-liqSoothsayer τ=0.85 near-liq

HOODx30/40$84.71$84.28-50.8✓ in / 1154.8✓ in / 1494.4CautionLiquidate

8 of 8 xStocks reach a different near-liquidation classification under Soothsayer τ=0.85 vs Kamino-incumbent on this weekend. Coverage context: Soothsayer τ=0.85 = 8/8, Soothsayer τ=0.95 = 8/8. This is a single-week illustration; the real comparison is the cross-week false-liquidation and missed-risk rate under the same reserve parameters.

LTV-gap breach analysis

For a borrower originated at the LTV ceiling, the trigger drop is (max_ltv / liq_threshold − 1) × Friday close. Below that price the new LTV exceeds the liquidation threshold. Each method flags if its lower bound sits at or below the trigger price; the realized move breaches if Monday open is at or below it. The 2×2 of (flagged, realized): matched / preemptive / missed / silent_safe.

SymbolTrigger drop (bps)Realized (bps)Realized breach?Kamino-incumbentSoothsayer τ=0.85Soothsayer τ=0.95Pyth regularSimple heuristic
SPYx-266.7-10.8—✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe⚠ preemptive✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe
QQQx-277.8-7.2—✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe⚠ preemptive✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe

HOODx-2500.0-50.8—✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe✓ silent_safe

0 of 8 xStocks realized a breach (Monday open below the LTV-gap trigger) this weekend. Per-method tally — Soothsayer τ=0.85: 0 matched / 0 preemptive / 0 missed / 8 silent_safe; Soothsayer τ=0.95: 0 matched / 2 preemptive / 0 missed / 6 silent_safe; Pyth regular: 0 matched / 0 preemptive / 0 missed / 8 silent_safe. Welfare-relevant ratios (recall, precision) emerge from the cross-week aggregate.
